---
applyTo: "_config.yml"
---

# `_config.yml` Instructions

This is the root Jekyll configuration. It controls site identity, the metadata CSV pointer, org branding, and build behavior. Changes here affect the entire site.

## URL Settings

```yaml
url:       # Production domain, e.g. https://username.github.io — leave blank for local dev
baseurl:   # Sub-path if collection is not at root, e.g. /digital/my-collection — leave blank if at root
source-code: https://github.com/your-org/your-repo
```


## Site Settings

```yaml
title:       # Appears in navbar, browser tab, and meta tags
tagline:     # Short phrase — appears in banner and home page
description: # Keep under 160 characters — appears in search result previews and meta tags
keywords:    # Semicolon-separated, e.g. "history; pacific northwest; photographs"
author:      # GitHub username or person/org name — appears in meta markup
```

## Collection Settings

```yaml
# CRITICAL: filename of the active metadata CSV in _data/, NO .csv extension
metadata: my-collection-metadata
```

This single value drives the entire site. Every page, visualization, and item page reads `site.data[site.metadata]`. **Never** include `.csv`. Changing this value switches the entire collection.

### Optional: Custom Page Generation

Only needed when overriding CB defaults or generating from more than one metadata CSV:

```yaml
page_gen:
  - data: 'my-collection-metadata'   # CSV filename without .csv
    template: 'item'                  # layout in _layouts/item/
    name: 'objectid'                  # column to use as output filename
    dir: 'items'                      # output directory
    extension: 'html'
    filter: 'objectid'                # only generate pages where this column is non-empty
```

## Organization Settings

```yaml
organization-name: "My Library"
organization-link: https://library.example.edu/
organization-logo-banner: https://example.edu/logo-dark.png   # shown in top banner (use dark version)
organization-logo-nav:    https://example.edu/logo-white.png  # shown in navbar (use light/white version)
```

Leave logo fields blank or delete them to remove logos from the UI.

## Robots / SEO

```yaml
# Uncomment to block all search engine indexing (e.g. for staging sites):
# noindex: true
```

Analytics and OG/Schema meta tags only appear when building with `JEKYLL_ENV=production`.

## Build Settings

```yaml
lib-assets: /assets/lib   # path to vendored JS/CSS libraries — only change for CDN delivery
exclude: [docs/, Rakefile, ...]   # files/folders excluded from Jekyll build output
sass:
  style: compressed   # compressed = minified CSS; expanded = readable CSS (useful for debugging)
```

Do not modify `exclude` unless you are intentionally adding or removing files from the build output.

## What NOT to Do Here

- Do not add custom Liquid variables or page data here — use `_data/theme.yml` instead
- Do not set `collections:` — item pages are generated by the CB plugin, not Jekyll collections
- Do not configure permalinks globally — item page URLs are controlled by `cb_page_gen.rb`
